message:
fix(update): preserve slashes in non-flat origin/HEAD branch names

Strip the literal `refs/remotes/origin/` prefix with shortest-match `#`
rather than longest-match `##*/`. The latter would corrupt branch names
containing slashes (e.g. an origin/HEAD pointing at `release/v1` would
become just `v1`, sending fetch/pull at the wrong ref).

Theoretical for ACFS today (primary branches are flat `main`/`master`)
but worth hardening since the symbolic-ref output format is documented
to include the full ref name.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -3391,7 +3391,10 @@ update_acfs_self() {
     local _origin_head_ref=""
     _origin_head_ref=$(git -C "$ACFS_REPO_ROOT" symbolic-ref --quiet refs/remotes/origin/HEAD 2>/dev/null) || true
     if [[ -n "$_origin_head_ref" ]]; then
-        remote_branch="${_origin_head_ref##*/}"
+        # Strip the literal `refs/remotes/origin/` prefix. Use shortest-match
+        # `#` rather than longest-match `##*/` so branch names containing
+        # slashes (e.g. `release/v1`) survive intact.
+        remote_branch="${_origin_head_ref#refs/remotes/origin/}"
     fi
 
     # Only auto-update when the local branch matches a primary remote branch
